business.headline: Quant funds or traditional mutual funds: What works better for Indians today?Quant brings scalability, discipline, and the power of data. Traditional brings context, adaptability, and judgement. As markets evolve and technology gets more deeply embedded in how money is managed, the investors who do well will not be the ones who pick sides.

Can India handle oil over $100? What it means for growth, inflation, marketsThe West Asia conflict has pushed up crude prices and exposed India's dependence on energy imports. How long the war lasts will decide the hit to growth, inflation, the rupee and markets.

Customers Bank CEO uses AI clone on earnings call to signal automation pushCustomers Bank CEO Sam Sidhu said an AI clone delivered part of the lender's earnings call. The moment underlined the bank's wider push to use OpenAI tools across lending, onboarding and operations.

8th Pay Commission meeting: Inside details of closed-door talks on key pay demandsI visited the 8th Pay Commission office to find out what was on the agenda of the closed-door consultations that began in Delhi yesterday. Here are the exclusive details.

Sensex, Nifty open higher despite oil prices hovering above $110; Maruti up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 363.81 points to 77,250.72, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 119.70 points to 24,115.40 as of 9:30 am.

Auto stocks in focus after Nitin Gadkari says 'no future' for petrol, dieselNitin Gadkari said there is no future for diesel and petrol engines and asked automakers to shift towards cleaner alternatives. His remarks put auto stocks in focus as investors weigh opportunities in electric buses, biofuels and higher-quality vehicles.

UAE exits OPEC: What it means for oil prices, global supply and IndiaThe Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is a group of major oil-producing nations formed in 1960 by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and Venezuela.

Government rules out fuel price hike after assembly polls conclude tomorrowDespite soaring global crude prices and heavy losses for fuel retailers, the government rules out any immediate hike in petrol and diesel rates, even as panic buying and supply concerns emerge in some states.

UAE exits oil production groups OPEC, OPEC+ amid global energy crisisThe decision, confirmed on Tuesday, delivers a big setback to the oil-producing alliance and its de facto leader, Saudi Arabia. The UAE, long seen as a major and reliable member, stepping away now risks weakening coordination within the bloc.

From power bills to food prices: How the heatwave is hitting your walletA heatwave is wreaking havoc across India, and it is starting to hit your wallet, pushing up electricity bills, food prices and everyday expenses through rising demand and supply disruptions.

Sensex, Nifty slip amid RBI norms, global cues; banks lead declineBenchmark indices ended lower on Wednesday as banking stocks fell after the RBI finalised key lending norms. Caution ahead of the US Fed decision, weak Asian cues and elevated oil prices kept investors on edge.

Are India's IT giants struggling to keep pace with global AI boom?The sector that defined India's growth story is entering a phase of uncertainty. India's biggest wealth creator is no longer the market's safest bet. The IT story isn't over, but the rules of the game have clearly changed.

Airlines send fuel price SOS to government, warn of shutdown in operationsIn a letter to the Civil Aviation Ministry, the Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents carriers such as Air India, IndiGo and SpiceJet, warned that the sector is facing "extreme stress" due to soaring aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices.

Snabbit raises $56 million as instant home services race heats upSnabbit has raised $56 million to expand its instant home services business across India. The round underlines growing investor confidence in organising a vast but still informal market.

Got this HR email on salary reimbursements? It could help you save more taxIf you have recently received an email from your office's HR department on salary reimbursements, it may be worth a closer look. It could influence how part of your salary is taxed.

Crude oil at $110: Why it is a wake-up call for India and the worldCrude oil has surged towards $110 a barrel as markets react to war-linked supply fears in West Asia. The rise matters for India and beyond because it can feed into fuel, inflation, imports and household costs.

Up nearly 40% in 2 days: What's fuelling this pharma stock's rallyCohance Lifesciences shares surged after the company named Umang Vohra as Executive Chairman and Group CEO. The move lifted expectations of faster growth, stronger execution and bigger scale in its CDMO business.

Six brokerages, six buy ratings: The story that market is missing about PaytmTarget prices set by Bernstein, Goldman Sachs, Jefferies, Bank of America, Investec and Emkay Global range from 1,250 to 1,500, pointing to an upside of as much as 31% from its Monday close of 1,138.
8th Pay Commission website down as key Delhi meetings set to beginThe 8th Pay Commission website appeared inaccessible as consultations with employee unions were set to begin in Delhi.

8th Pay Commission: Will govt employee salaries rise as key talks begin today?The 8th Pay Commission will begin a three-day consultation with employee unions in New Delhi. The talks will shape decisions on pay, allowances, pensions and the closely watched fitment factor.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 41: Ranveer's film Rs 8 crore away from new milestone Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has reached Rs 1,780.82 crore worldwide after 41 days in cinemas. The Ranveer Singh-starrer is now Rs 8 crore short of overtaking Baahubali 2's global total.

Yash postpones Toxic release again, new date to be announced soon Yash has postponed the release of Toxic from its earlier June 4 date. He said the completed film is being held back to align a wider global rollout.

The story behind Love Story: Taylor Swift reveals why she wrote the song at 17 Taylor Swift said she wrote Love Story at 17 while upset with her parents for stopping her from dating an older boy. The singer later dated Jake Gyllenhaal, who was 10 years older than her.

Karisma Kapoor asks paps not to zoom in while clicking her on set, internet reacts Karisma Kapoor asked paparazzi not to zoom in while posing outside the set of India's Best Dancer Season 5. The brief exchange triggered online discussion about privacy and basic boundaries in public spaces.

Aditya Dhar planning new big film with Ranveer Singh. Check details Aditya Dhar has begun developing a new film and is in talks with Ranveer Singh for the lead. If the project moves ahead, it will mark another ambitious collaboration after Dhurandhar.

Mukesh Chhabra on trolling of Ramayana, Dhurandhar: Negativity is a bit too much Casting director Mukesh Chhabra addressed the pre-release backlash around Ramayana and Dhurandhar. He reflected on online negativity and the growing tendency for audiences to "over-judge" films these days.

One love: Diljit cheers Jimmy Fallon in thank-you note for celebrating his culture Diljit Dosanjh thanked Jimmy Fallon after his second appearance on The Tonight Show and shared a backstage Bhangra moment with the host. The exchange underscored his push to represent Indian culture globally as he also spoke about the emotional weight of his Vancouver concert.

Michael box office: Film moonwalks into Rs 25 crore in India, sets UK record Jaafar Jackson's Michael crossed Rs 25 crore gross in India on day five. The rise made it April's highest-grossing Hollywood release in the country ahead of fresh competition.

Taylor Swift Vienna concert terror plot: Austrian man pleads guilty as trial begins An Austrian man accused of planning an attack on a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna has pleaded guilty as his trial opened. The case has renewed focus on the foiled threat that led to the cancellation of three shows and left fans devastated.

Superstar cameo to veteran returns: 5 reasons to watch Dhruva Sarja's KD Prem's KD: The Devil, led by Dhruva Sarja, is set for a long-delayed theatrical release on April 30. Interest remains around its ensemble cast, surprise cameo and 1970s gangster backdrop despite mixed promotional response and controversy.

Diljit Dosanjh invokes Komagata Maru after 55,000 Indians pack Vancouver concert Diljit Dosanjh has said that his Vancouver concert moved him deeply. He linked the packed stadium to the Komagata Maru history and what it meant for Indians in Canada.

Pokiri, Darling, Ghilli: Why iconic South blockbusters deserve pan-India re-run Re-released South superhits are drawing huge crowds and turning screenings into fan-led celebrations. The trend is reviving older titles, but fans outside South India are missing all the fun.

Anne Hathaway denies thinner models being fired from The Devil Wears Prada 2 set Anne Hathaway has reacted to speculations about skinnier models being fired after she pushed for size inclusivity on The Devil Wears Prada 2 set. Her clarification followed a viral reaction to Meryl Streep's account and social media claims about job losses.

Filmmaker Dan Reed slams Michael for whitewashing MJ's alleged child abuse Emmy-winning filmmaker Dan Reed has criticised Antoine Fuqua's biopic, Michael, for presenting a sanitised account of Michael Jackson's relationship with children. He slammed the film for not showing any of the alleged abuse of children.

Aamir Khan feels 'complete' after meeting Gauri, opens up on ex-wives Reena, Kiran Aamir Khan said he feels 'mukammal' (complete) after meeting girlfriend Gauri Spratt and revealed that she has brought peace in his life. He also reflected on his marriages to Reena Dutta and Kiran Rao, and spoke about his biggest regret.

Did Rupali Ganguly call out Alia Bhatt's leather look at Milan Fashion Week? Rupali Ganguly stirred controversy online after resharing a video that criticised Alia Bhatt for allegedly wearing animal-derived leather at Milan Fashion Week.

I'm a big SRK fan: Australian minister Tony Burke calls Om Shanti Om his favourite Shah Rukh Khan has found a fan in Australian Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, who revealed that he deeply admires the Bollywood star. Burke also shared that Om Shanti Om is his all-time favourite film.

Bhooth Bangla: Wamiqa Gabbi gets emotional over her first Rs 100 crore film Wamiqa Gabbi shared a thank-you note after Bhooth Bangla crossed the Rs 100 crore mark. The milestone marks her first Rs 100 crore film and comes as the release holds steady at the box office despite mixed reviews.

Neha Dhupia, Angad Bedi share how unplanned pregnancy changed their love story Neha Dhupia and Angad Bedi opened up about their unconventional love story and revealed how an unplanned pregnancy changed their relationship. The couple shared that their daughter Mehr became the reason they decided to get married in 2018.

general news.headline: BJP alleges EVM tampering in Jahangir Khan's constituency, demands repollingThe West Bengal Election Commissioner has warned that any reports of EVM buttons being taped must be verified and recorded, and if found to be true, those booths will undergo a repoll.
You'd be speaking French without us: King Charles roasts Trump with history jabBlending humour with history, King Charles III turned a White House toast into a witty reminder of Anglo-American ties.

Mamata Banerjee accuses BJP of rigging Bengal polls, Suvendu Adhikari hits backMamata Banerjee accused the BJP of rigging and slammed Election Commission observers, calling them 'terrorists'. BJP leader. In response, Suvendu Adhikari alleged that she was moving with 'goonda elements' to intimidate voters and claimed Hindu voters were being threatened during polling in Bhabanipur, escalating political tensions.

Iran to run out of space to store oil in weeks? What happens then?A report by research firm Kpler has revealed that Iran is running out of space to store its oil. If storage reaches its limits within weeks, Iran might have to shut down its oil production operations. The fuel crisis can deepen as oil exports have been choked by the US naval blockade near the Strait of Hormuz.

8th Pay Commission meeting: Inside details of closed-door talks on key pay demandsI visited the 8th Pay Commission office to find out what was on the agenda of the closed-door consultations that began in Delhi yesterday. Here are the exclusive details.

Delhi teen calls father to say he's headed home after IPL, dies before finishing callA 14-year-old boy, who was killed along with his cousin in a hit-and-run case in Delhi, was on a phone call with his father when the accident occurred, telling him he would reach home soon.

How a typo by cops helped Meghalaya honeymoon murder accused secure bailA Meghalaya court granted Sonam Raghuvanshi bail after finding serious flaws in her arrest documents. She is accused of murdering her husband while the couple was on their honeymoon last year.

Live: Bengal phase 2 polls see 39.97% voter turnout till 11 amWest Bengal Assembly Election 2026 Live Updates: Voting is underway in the second and final phase of the West Bengal Assembly elections on Wednesday, with 142 constituencies across the politically crucial southern region going to polls. The phase covers key districts including Kolkata, Howrah, North and South 24 Parganas, Nadia, Hooghly and Purba Bardhaman, areas that form the Trinamool Congress's traditional stronghold. Bhabanipur is the focal point, where Suvendu Adhikari faces Mamata Banerjee in a high-stakes rematch of their 2021 Nandigram clash. Polling began at 7 am and will continue till 6 pm. After strong turnout in the first phase, a similar response is expected today. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

Mumbai woman accidentally leaves house unlocked, delivery man notices it. And then..A Mumbai woman accidentally left the front door of her house open, but what a delivery agent did after he noticed it is something you don't come across every day.

AI could kill us all, we don't want to be in Terminator movie: Elon Musk at OpenAI trialElon Musk used his OpenAI trial testimony to warn that AI could become dangerous if left unchecked. He said the world should avoid a "Terminator" future and build AI that benefits humanity.

BCCI urged to act after video of Riyan Parag vaping during IPL match sparks backlashIPL 2026, PBKS vs RR: A viral video appears to show Riyan Parag vaping in the Rajasthan Royals dressing room after the Punjab Kings match, triggering calls for BCCI action and renewed scrutiny over team discipline.

Bengal poll violence flares, nearly 40% turnout amid allegations of EVM tampering Live UpdatesWest Bengal votes in the second and final phase of its Assembly election with the BJP challenging the TMC for 142 seats. The result will show whether the BJP can breach the TMC's strongest base or whether Mamata Banerjee stays on for a fourth term.

Rain and storm lower temperatures: How long will the rain relief last?This brief respite highlights how erratically the weather has been behaving in 2026, with the overall trend pointing towards potentially record-breaking summer days ahead.

UAE exits OPEC: What it means for oil prices, global supply and IndiaThe Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) is a group of major oil-producing nations formed in 1960 by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia and Venezuela.

Yash postpones Toxic release again, new date to be announced soonYash has postponed the release of Toxic from its earlier June 4 date. He said the completed film is being held back to align a wider global rollout.

Video: Massive fire breaks out at Ghaziabad society, fire tenders rush to spotThe incident occurred in the densely populated Indirapuram locality, raising concerns among residents as emergency teams work to bring the situation under control.

Asuran, Karnan and Kara: Why Dhanush's saviour roles never get happy victoriesDhanush has so far perfected portraying the flawed and wounded man as a saviour, far removed from the larger-than-life element many superstars give to the image. His new film, Kara, scheduled to release on April 30, promises to extend the arc, underlining his on-screen battles with the system.

EC swings into action after row over UP Singham's remarks against TMC candidateAn EC-ordered transfer, a viral 'Singham' warning, protests, and a serious complaint alleging night raids - the Falta controversy deepens ahead of April 29 polling in West Bengal.

Sooryavanshi topples IPL records again: Races past Russell, Abhishek SharmaIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed 43 off 16 balls and became the fastest to 400 runs in an IPL season as Rajasthan Royals beat Punjab Kings.

Is UAE's fight against Pakistan-Saudi axis out in the open?The UAE sought back $3.5 billion in loans it gave to Pakistan. Now, it has exited oil cartel OPEC, which would be a blow to its de facto leader Saudi Arabia. Is this UAE's open war against the Saudi-Pakistan nexus?

No sweets, no ghee: India's milk industry is fighting an invisible warIn this week's edition of Climate On My Plate, the focus shifts to how India's dependence on milk can be undermined if the world continues to warm, reducing how much milk is supplied and how expensive it might become.

Harmony must prevail: Akhilesh visits BJP MLA injured while burning his effigyAkhilesh Yadav visited Anupama Jaiswal at a Lucknow hospital after she suffered burns during a Bahraich protest. During his visit, he met her family members and enquired about her condition. The visit was appreciated by Jaiswal's husband, Ashok Jaiswal, who described it as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."
Bengaluru Congress leader assaults lover's minor children, Pocso case filedBengaluru Congress leader, Anand Naidu, allegedly assaulted the minor children of his lover, who is married to another man.

Pune cops issue 'Leave India' notice to 3 US nationals for promoting ChristianityPune police have told three US nationals to leave India after alleging they promoted Christianity while on tourist visas. The action followed an inquiry into pamphlet distribution and alleged religious preaching in Shukrawar Peth.

sports.headline: Punjab Kings' bowling woes? Shreyas, coach split on pace attack concerns IPL 2026: Punjab Kings' unbeaten run ended despite posting 222, exposing concerns about their struggling bowling attack. While Shreyas Iyer blamed poor execution, the fast-bowling coach argued conditions were largely responsible and urged patience.

BCCI urged to act after video of Riyan Parag vaping during IPL match sparks backlash IPL 2026, PBKS vs RR: A viral video appears to show Riyan Parag vaping in the Rajasthan Royals dressing room after the Punjab Kings match, triggering calls for BCCI action and renewed scrutiny over team discipline.

Old Babar Azam back? Pakistan star makes bold claim after record PSL century Babar Azam hit 103 off 59 balls as Peshawar Zalmi beat Islamabad United by 70 runs in the Qualifier on Tuesday to reach the PSL 2026 final. Babar made a bold claim after winning the Player of the Match award for his second hundred of the season.

Desperate Mumbai hope to break shackles vs resurgent SRH at Wankhede IPL 2026, MI vs SRH: A struggling Mumbai Indians host third-placed Sunrisers Hyderabad on Wednesday, April 29. Mumbai are sweating on Rohit Sharma's fitness as they hope to return to winning ways in the Indian Premier League.

Play of the Day: How the Don arrived and fixed Rajasthan's biggest crisis IPL 2016: Donovan Ferreira stepped up when Rajasthan Royals needed it most against Punjab Kings. The middle order finally delivered, helping them chase 223 and hand Shreyas Iyer's men their first defeat of the season on Tuesday.

I am not old: Yashasvi Jaiswal's hilarious response to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi question IPL 2026, PBKS vs RR: Yashasvi Jaiswal laughed off being called the senior partner after Rajasthan Royals' successful chase in Punjab on Tuesdady. He praised 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying he enjoys batting with the record-breaking teenager.

PSG vs Bayern instant classic: Paris secure slender lead in record-breaking epic Champions League, PSG vs Bayern Munich: In a masterclass of clinical precision and tactical poise, Paris Saint-Germain edged Bayern Munich 5-4 on Tuesday. The historic nine-goal symphony of elite European football leaves the semi-final perfectly balanced ahead of a monumental Munich decider.

Riyan Parag seen vaping in dressing room: Can IPL punish RR captain? A live broadcast clip appeared to show Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag vaping in the dressing room during the chase against Punjab Kings. The footage went viral online and raised questions over possible IPL action.

Shreyas Iyer blames bowlers after Punjab Kings lose first game in IPL 2026 Punjab Kings slipped to their first defeat of the season as Rajasthan Royals chased down 223 in Mullanpur. Shreyas Iyer said the target was strong on a tacky pitch, but his side failed to execute their bowling plans.

Sooryavanshi topples IPL records again: Races past Russell, Abhishek Sharma IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed 43 off 16 balls and became the fastest to 400 runs in an IPL season as Rajasthan Royals beat Punjab Kings.

Mission possible. Rajasthan Royals hand Punjab Kings first defeat in IPL 2026 Rajasthan Royals became the first team in the Indian Premier League's 2026 edition to beat Punjab Kings. Finishers Donovan Ferreira and Shubham Dubey put together an epic fightback to pull off a clinical chase in Mullanpur.

Will Rohit Sharma play vs SRH? Big update from MI keeps fans hopefulIPL2026: Mumbai Indians will take a call on Rohit Sharma's availability for the Sunrisers Hyderabad match at the toss. His potential return could ease the side's unsettled top-order concerns amid other selection issues.

FIFA boss asks Canada for higher security than sitting PM. Country refusesFIFA president Gianni Infantino reportedly requested a security arrangement in Canada that exceeded the protection given to Prime Minister Mark Carney. However, Vancouver police turned down the proposal.

Did WFI lie on Vinesh Phogat registration row? Fans spot big detailVinesh Phogat's registration for the National Open Ranking tournament triggered a fresh row after a viral screenshot showed a late-night entry on the WFI portal. The timestamp drew scrutiny because it matched the night she complained of delays.

Punjab Kings drop Shashank Singh vs RR: Injury concern or fielding fallout?IPL 2026: Punjab Kings left out Shashank Singh against Rajasthan Royals, officially citing an injury, though the decision comes amid a run of costly fielding errors.

BCCI's big NorthEast push explained: PM Modi inaugurates 6 indoor facilitiesBCCI has expanded into the NorthEast with six new indoor academies. The move aims to create year-round training and widen the talent base.

England name ageing squad for Women's T20 World Cup, include 18-year-old spinnerEngland cricket team have named an experienced 15-player squad for the 2026 Women's T20 World Cup, led by Nat Sciver-Brunt with 18-year-old spinner Tilly Corteen-Coleman as the standout pick alongside. They will play India and New Zealand in a white ball series prior to their opening World Cup game against Sri Lanka on 12 June at Edgbaston.

Virat Kohli's U19 India teammate now an IPL 2026 umpire: Who is he?IPL 2026: Virat Kohli reunited with former U19 teammate Ajitesh Argal after DC vs RCB. The now-umpire's full-circle moment has gone viral.

PSG hosts a defiant Bayern Munich in the UEFA Champions League semi-finalsParis Saint-Germain host Bayern Munich in a high-stakes UEFA Champions League semi-final first leg, with both sides eyeing a spot in the final after dominant runs this season. Bayern chase a treble under Vincent Kompany, while PSG, led by Luis Enrique, aim to continue their trophy-laden campaign after a commanding path to the last four.

Has absence from international cricket worked in Bhuvneshwar Kumar's favour?IPL 2026: Mitchell McClenaghan said Bhuvneshwar Kumar's success comes from repeating proven methods, not reinvention. The 36-year-old fast bowler is leading the wicket-takers' charts with 14 scalps in eight matches this season,

Bruno Fernandes' war cry to Manchester United team before Brentford win goes viralManchester United edged closer to Champions League qualification with a 2–1 win over Brentford, powered by Bruno Fernandes' assist and viral rally. Fernandes' leadership and 19th assist of the season has driven United's surge under Michael Carrick to the brink of a top-three finish.

Kohli says speaking in school is awkward, but gives golden message to studentsVirat Kohli delivered a golden message to young cricketers in Delhi as he inaugurated a branch of his childhood coach's academy on Tuesday, April 28.

Even in IPL rush, Virat Kohli makes time for childhood coach, inaugurates his academyVirat Kohli stayed back in Delhi to inaugurate his childhood coach Rajkumar Sharma's new academy branch at DPS RK Puram on Tuesday, highlighting their long-standing bond. He will travel to Ahmedabad for Royal Challengers Bengaluru's next match against Gujarat Titans on Thursday.

Manchester United close to Champions League spot, but still work to do: CarrickManchester United interim manager Michael Carrick remains grounded despite his side edging closer to securing a spot in next year's Champions League. United need just two more points to secure the spot after their 2-1 win over Brentford.

Mind your business: Kris Srikkanth grills Hemang Badani over Fleming-Dhoni jibesIPL 2026, DC vs RCB: Kris Srikkanth tore into Hemang Badani after Delhi Capitals' nine-wicket loss to Royal Challengers Bengaluru, citing his old remarks on Stephen Fleming. The attack intensified scrutiny of Delhi's tactics, team calls and familiar mid-season slide after three straight defeats.

How critics made Shreyas solve his short-ball issue: Wanted to prove them wrongShreyas Iyer said criticism over his short-ball weakness pushed him to improve as the PBKS captain opened up on fixing the issue and becoming one of IPL's best finishers.

Off target: Internet erupts over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi question to Manu BhakerAt an event in New Delhi celebrating 75 years of Indian shooting excellence, Manu Bhaker was asked about cricket prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. While Bhaker's response was a masterclass in maturity, the question itself has drawn sharp criticism, reigniting the debate over the Indian media's enduring obsession with cricket.

Wrestling Federation deliberately blocking my return: Vinesh PhogatVinesh Phogat claimed the Wrestling Federation of India is deliberately trying to stop her comeback by blocking her National Open Ranking Tournament registration ahead of the Asian Games trials.

IPL 2026, PBKS vs RR preview: Iyer's unbeaten Punjab face Sooryavanshi stormPBKS bring an unbeaten run and record-breaking batting into a high-scoring clash vs RR. All eyes, though, remain on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's red-hot form.

Sindhu's heartbreaking loss triggers India's Uber Cup exit, blanked 0-5 by ChinaPV Sindhu lost to Wang Zhiyi in a thrilling three-game contest as India suffered a 0-5 defeat to China and was eliminated from the Uber Cup 2026. Sindhu squandered the win against Wang, despite leading in the decider.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
